claim_gate.reason: Naive fixed-degree disjoint multilinear sketches are ruled out under the margin/MSE contract. The most important nonlinear classes remain open and require separate dependence or circuit analyses.

estimator_classes.finding: Signed sums of independent uniform labels remain uniform; degree r multiplies the block second moment by 4^r.
estimator_classes.open_obligation: None inside the stated one-kernel disjoint-block MSE model; alter the estimator class.

estimator_classes.finding: The disjoint-block lower bound does not control cancellation among overlapping tuples or degenerate Hoeffding projections.
estimator_classes.open_obligation: Bound every Hoeffding projection or construct a degenerate kernel with uniform bucket margin and polynomial evaluation.

estimator_classes.finding: A family of scores can route adaptively without any one score separating the complete bucket from its complement.
estimator_classes.open_obligation: Charge the number of scores, shared-sample dependence, per-node margin, and worst-reflection error.

estimator_classes.finding: Enumerating all m^r tuples is superpolynomial once r grows with n unless an implicit contraction is proved.
estimator_classes.open_obligation: Give a polynomial-description contraction or factorization and audit whether it recreates an N-sized spectrum.

estimator_classes.finding: Classical postmeasurement multilinear bounds do not constrain entangled measurements before local X/Y readout.
estimator_classes.open_obligation: Provide a polynomial-size measurement circuit, exact f=1 robustness, and full lattice composition.

falsifiers_triggered: A signed sum of any fixed number of independent uniform DCP labels is still uniform and does not synthesize chosen-label access.
A single degree-r multilinear kernel on disjoint blocks obeys the same response Parseval bound and incurs a 4^r outcome second moment.
Increasing degree makes the certified disjoint-block record cost worse, not better.
The theorem does not cover overlapping U-statistics because dependent tuples can have degenerate Hoeffding projections.
The theorem does not cover adaptive score families or collective quantum measurements.

observation_model.aggregate_label: K=sum_j sigma_j k_j mod N for fixed public signs sigma_j in {+1,-1}
observation_model.degree_r_kernel: Z=A(k_1,...,k_r) uses y_j for positive signs and conjugate(y_j) for negative signs, on disjoint blocks
observation_model.records: independent (k_j,y_j), uniform k_j in Z_N, E[y_j|k_j]=chi_d(k_j), |y_j|=2
observation_model.target: one bucket versus its complement with a uniform expected-score margin

summary: Certified 120 degree-indexed disjoint-block rows through degree 8. Joint polynomial rows=0; open estimator classes=3.

theorem.aggregate_uniformity: K is uniform for every fixed degree and sign pattern
theorem.record_lower_bound: m>=r ceil(16(4^r N-1)S(N-S)/N^2) for disjoint blocks and uniform MSE<=gamma^2/4
theorem.scope: one multilinear kernel averaged over independent disjoint blocks
